member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.TransactionStatus
member.summary: A code that indicates the transaction status. Value is: Status code Description D PayPal or merchant rules denied the transaction. P The transaction is pending. The transaction was created but waits for another payment process to complete, such as an ACH transaction, before the status changes to S. S The transaction successfully completed without a denial and after any pending statuses. V A successful transaction was fully reversed and funds were refunded to the original sender.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.TransactionSubject
member.summary: The subject of payment. The payer passes this value to the payee. The payer controls this data through the interface through which he or she sends the data.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.TransactionNote
member.summary: A special note that the payer passes to the payee. Might contain special customer requests, such as shipping instructions.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.PaymentTrackingId
member.summary: The payment tracking ID, which is a unique ID that partners specify to either get information about a payment or request a refund.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.BankReferenceId
member.summary: The bank reference ID. The bank provides this value for an ACH transaction.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.EndingBalance
member.summary: The currency and amount for a financial transaction, such as a balance or payment due.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.AvailableBalance
member.summary: The currency and amount for a financial transaction, such as a balance or payment due.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.InvoiceId
member.summary: The invoice ID that is sent by the merchant with the transaction. Note: If an invoice ID was sent with the capture request, the value is reported. Otherwise, the invoice ID of the authorizing transaction is reported.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.CustomField
member.summary: The merchant-provided custom text. Note: Usually, this field includes the unique ID for payments made with MassPay type transaction.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.ProtectionEligibility
member.summary: Indicates whether the transaction is eligible for protection. Value is: 01. Eligible. 02. Not eligible 03. Partially eligible.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.CreditTerm
member.summary: The credit term. The time span covered by the installment payments as expressed in the term length plus the length time unit code.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.CreditTransactionalFee
member.summary: The currency and amount for a financial transaction, such as a balance or payment due.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.CreditPromotionalFee
member.summary: The currency and amount for a financial transaction, such as a balance or payment due.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.AnnualPercentageRate
member.summary: The percentage, as a fixed-point, signed decimal number. For example, define a 19.99% interest rate as `19.99`.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.PaymentMethodType
member.summary: The payment method that was used for a transaction. Value is PUI, installment, or mEFT. Note: Appears only for pay upon invoice (PUI), installment, and mEFT transactions. Merchants and partners in the EMEA region can use this attribute to note transactions that attract turn-over tax.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.InstrumentType
member.summary: A high-level classification of the type of financial instrument that was used to fund a payment. The pattern is not provided because the value is defined by an external party. E.g. PAYPAL, CREDIT_CARD, DEBIT_CARD, APPLE_PAY, BANK , VENMO ,Pay Upon Invoice, Pay Later or Alternative Payment Methods (APM).

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.TransactionInformation.InstrumentSubType
member.summary: A finer-grained classification of the financial instrument that was used to fund a payment. For example, `Visa card` or a `Mastercard` for a credit card, BANKCARD ,DISCOVER etc. The pattern is not provided because the value is defined by an external party.

member.summary: Pay after use, non-recurring post-paid, variable amount, irregular frequency. Deferred.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsagePattern.RecurringPrepaid
member.summary: Pay upfront fixed or variable amount on a fixed date before the goods/service is delivered. RecurringPrepaid.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsagePattern.RecurringPostpaid
member.summary: Pay on a fixed date based on usage or consumption after the goods/service is delivered. RecurringPostpaid.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsagePattern.ThresholdPrepaid
member.summary: Charge payer when the set amount is reached or monthly billing cycle, whichever comes first, before the goods/service is delivered. ThresholdPrepaid.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsagePattern.ThresholdPostpaid
member.summary: Charge payer when the set amount is reached or monthly billing cycle, whichever comes first, after the goods/service is delivered. ThresholdPostpaid.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsagePattern.SubscriptionPrepaid
member.summary: Subscription plan where the "amount due" and the "billing frequency" are fixed, and there is no defined duration with the payment due before the good/service is delivered. SubscriptionPrepaid.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsagePattern.SubscriptionPostpaid
member.summary: Subscription plan where the "amount due" and the "billing frequency" are fixed, and there is no defined duration with the payment due after the goods/services are delivered. SubscriptionPostpaid.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsagePattern.UnscheduledPrepaid
member.summary: Unscheduled card on file plan where the merchant can bill buyer upfront based on an agreed logic, but "amount due" and "frequency" can vary. Inclusive of automatic reload plans. UnscheduledPrepaid.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsagePattern.UnscheduledPostpaid
member.summary: Unscheduled card on file plan where the merchant can bill buyer based on an agreed logic, but "amount due" and "frequency" can vary. Inclusive of automatic reload plans. UnscheduledPostpaid.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsagePattern.InstallmentPrepaid
member.summary: Merchant-managed installment plan when the "amount" to be paid and the "billing frequency" are fixed, but there is a defined number of payments with the payment due before the good/service is delivered. InstallmentPrepaid.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsagePattern.InstallmentPostpaid
member.summary: Merchant-managed installment plan when the "amount" to be paid and the "billing frequency" are fixed, but there is a defined number of payments with the payment due after the goods/services are delivered. InstallmentPostpaid.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsagePattern._Unknown
member.summary: Unknown values will be mapped by this enum member.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsageType.Merchant
member.summary: The PayPal Payment Token will be used for future transaction directly with a merchant. Merchant.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.UsageType.Platform
member.summary: The PayPal Payment Token will be used for future transaction on a platform. A platform is typically a marketplace or a channel that a payer can purchase goods and services from multiple merchants. Platform.

member.summary: The label that overrides the business name in the PayPal account on the PayPal site. The pattern is defined by an external party and supports Unicode.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.VaultCardExperienceContext.Locale
member.summary: The [language tag](https://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47#section-2) for the language in which to localize the error-related strings, such as messages, issues, and suggested actions. The tag is made up of the [ISO 639-2 language code](https://www.loc.gov/standards/iso639-2/php/code_list.php), the optional [ISO-15924 script tag](https://www.unicode.org/iso15924/codelists.html), and the [ISO-3166 alpha-2 country code](/api/rest/reference/country-codes/) or [M49 region code](https://unstats.un.org/unsd/methodology/m49/).

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.VaultCardExperienceContext.ReturnUrl
member.summary: The URL where the customer is redirected after customer approves leaves the flow. It is a required field for contingency flows like PayPal wallet, 3DS.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.VaultCardExperienceContext.CancelUrl
member.summary: The URL where the customer is redirected after customer cancels or leaves the flow. It is a required field for contingency flows like PayPal wallet, 3DS.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.VaultCardExperienceContext.VaultInstruction
member.summary: DEPRECATED. Vault Instruction on action to be performed after a successful payer approval.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.VaultCardExperienceContext.UserAction
member.summary: User Action on action to be performed after a successful payer approval.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.VaultCardVerificationMethod.ScaWhenRequired
member.summary: When an authorization or transaction is attempted this option will return a contingency and HATEOAS link only when local regulations require strong customer authentication, (e.g. 3DS in countries and use cases where it is mandated). The API caller should redirect the payer to the link so that they can authenticate themselves. In all cases, when an authorization is requested the AVS/CVV results will be returned in the response. ScaWhenRequired.

member.name: F:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.VaultCardVerificationMethod.ScaAlways
member.summary: Selecting this option will attempt to force a strong customer authentication for the authorization/transaction. In countries where SCA has been defined and implemented it will result in a contingency and HATEOAS link being returned. The API caller should redirect the payer to that link so that they can authenticate themselves against their issuing bank or other entity. As noted, the HATEOAS link is only available in all regions where strong authentication is supported, (e.g. in European countries where 3DS is live). Merchants can use this setting as an additional layer of security if they choose to. In all cases, when an authorization is requested the AVS/CVV results will be returned in the response. ScaAlways.

member.summary: Unknown values will be mapped by this enum member.

member.summary: Create multiple payment tokens for the same payer, merchant/platform combination. Use this when the customer has not logged in at merchant/platform. The payment token thus generated, can then also be used to create the customer account at merchant/platform. Use this also when multiple payment tokens are required for the same payer, different customer at merchant/platform. This helps to identify customers distinctly even though they may share the same PayPal account. This only applies to PayPal payment source.

member.summary: The usage type associated with the Venmo payment token.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.VenmoWalletVaultAttributes.CustomerType
member.summary: The customer type associated with the Venmo payment token. This is to indicate whether the customer acting on the merchant / platform is either a business or a consumer.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.VenmoWalletVaultAttributes.PermitMultiplePaymentTokens
member.summary: Create multiple payment tokens for the same payer, merchant/platform combination. Use this when the customer has not logged in at merchant/platform. The payment token thus generated, can then also be used to create the customer account at merchant/platform. Use this also when multiple payment tokens are required for the same payer, different customer at merchant/platform. This helps to identify customers distinctly even though they may share the same Venmo account.

member.summary: An API-caller-provided JSON Web Token (JWT) assertion that identifies the merchant. For details, see [PayPal-Auth-Assertion](/docs/api/reference/api-requests/#paypal-auth-assertion). member.summary: The server stores keys for 45 days.

member.name: P:PaypalServerSdk.Standard.Models.VoidPaymentInput.Prefer
member.summary: The preferred server response upon successful completion of the request. Value is: return=minimal. The server returns a minimal response to optimize communication between the API caller and the server. A minimal response includes the id, status and HATEOAS links. return=representation. The server returns a complete resource representation, including the current state of the resource.
